title: 10 best herbal decoctions for angina
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for angina are a natural remedy that combines various herbs to create a tea-like liquid that helps alleviate symptoms of angina, a condition characterized by chest pain or discomfort caused by reduced blood flow to the heart. These decoctions work by relaxing blood vessels, reducing inflammation, and improving circulation, which can help reduce frequency and severity of angina attacks. Examples of herbal decoctions that have shown promise in alleviating angina include ginkgo biloba, hawthorn berry, and garlic tea, which have been reported to improve exercise tolerance and overall quality of life for individuals with angina, allowing them to engage in daily activities without fear of debilitating pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn decoctions helps with angina because they contain flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) that have a positive effect on the cardiovascular system. These compounds help to relax blood vessels, improve blood flow to the heart, and reduce inflammation in the coronary arteries. By doing so, hawthorn decoctions can help to alleviate anginal symptoms such as chest pain and shortness of breath, reducing the frequency and severity of attacks. This natural remedy has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to support cardiovascular health and manage angina.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Hawthorn extracts contain cardiac glycosides, which help to improve heart function by increasing contractility and reducing symptoms of angina, such as chest pain.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in hawthorn decoctions have antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to angina development.
Oligomeric procyanidins (OPCs): OPCs in hawthorn extracts have vasodilatory effects, which help to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart muscle, reducing symptoms of angina.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Crataegus monogyna, particularly quercetin, rutin, and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to improve cardiovascular health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, thereby alleviating angina symptoms.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, including ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, in Crataegus monogyna have been shown to have vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to improve blood flow to the heart muscle and reduce the frequency and severity of angina attacks.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including chlorogenic and caffeic acids, in Crataegus monogyna have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, thereby reducing the risk of angina and other cardiovascular diseases.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce angina symptoms by improving cardiovascular health.
Barks: Rich in salicylates, which have anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular benefits that can help alleviate angina symptoms.
Fruits: Contain flavonoids and other antioxidants that may help improve cardiovascular health and reduce angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh c monogyna leaves and twigs in early morning when they are at their highest moisture level.
Dry the collected c monogyna leaves and twigs in a warm oven at 50c for 2 hours.
Measure 50 grams of dried c monogyna leaves and twigs and add them to 1 liter of cold water.
Bring the water to a boil and then simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with angina because they possess a unique combination of flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds that work synergistically to relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and reduce inflammation. The decoction's potent antioxidant properties also help to scavenge free radicals, which can damage heart tissue and contribute to anginal episodes. By reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular function, maidenhair tree decoctions may provide relief from the symptoms of angina, allowing individuals to live a more comfortable and active lifestyle.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic activity: Maidenhair tree decoctions contain cardiotonic compounds that help to improve cardiac function, reducing symptoms of angina by increasing blood flow to the heart.
Anti-platelet aggregation: The decoctions have been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation, which helps to reduce the risk of thrombosis and atherosclerosis, both common underlying causes of angina.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in maidenhair tree decoctions help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to the development of angina by damaging blood vessels and reducing blood flow.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol, Isorhapontigenin): These flavonoids help with angina by reducing oxidative stress, improving blood vessel function, and increasing nitric oxide production, ultimately leading to improved blood flow and reduced inflammation.

Bilobalide: This triterpene helps with angina by reducing inflammation, improving blood vessel function, and inhibiting platelet aggregation, which in turn reduces the risk of blood clots and improves blood flow to the heart.

Ginkgolides (Ginkgolide B): These diterpene lactones help with angina by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), a chemical involved in platelet aggregation and inflammation, which in turn reduces the risk of blood clots and improves blood flow to the heart.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to their medicinal properties.
Seeds: Seeds are used in decoctions for angina because they contain a high concentration of ginkgolic acid, which has been shown to have vasodilatory effects.
Roots: Roots are another part of Ginkgo biloba used in decoctions for angina, as they contain compounds that may help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and stems in quantities of 1 teaspoon per cup of water.
Combine the ginkgo biloba with 1 cup of boiling water and let it steep for 3 to 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container to remove solids.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze it for longer preservation.
Take 1 to 2 cups of the decoction per day to experience potential health benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng decoctions helps with angina because it has been shown to improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the frequency and severity of anginal attacks. The antioxidants and adaptogens present in ginseng decoctions help to relax blood vessels, increase oxygen delivery to the heart, and reduce inflammation. Additionally, ginseng decoctions have been found to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure, further contributing to their beneficial effects on cardiovascular health and alleviating symptoms of angina.
remedy_properties: Cardiac toning and vasodilation: Ginseng decoctions have been shown to improve blood flow to the heart, reducing symptoms of angina by increasing oxygenation of the cardiac tissue.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: Ginseng's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the coronary arteries, which can contribute to anginal episodes, while its antioxidants can protect against oxidative stress and damage to cardiovascular tissues.

Vasoprotective and hypotensive properties: Ginseng decoctions have been found to relax blood vessels, lower blood pressure, and improve vascular function, all of which can help alleviate symptoms of angina by reducing cardiac workload.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help with angina by improving cardiovascular function, reducing inflammation, and promoting vasodilation, which can increase blood flow to the heart and reduce oxygen demand.

Glycosides: These compounds, particularly ginsenoside Rb1, possess antioxidant properties that can help protect the heart from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, contributing to the management of angina.

Saponins: Specifically, ginsenoside Rg1 has been shown to have a protective effect on the cardiovascular system by reducing inflammation, improving cardiac function, and inhibiting platelet aggregation, all of which can contribute to the alleviation of angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The most used part of Panax ginseng for making decoctions to treat angina is its roots, as they contain the highest concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to improve cardiovascular health.
Stems: Stems of Panax ginseng are also used in decoctions for angina due to their high content of bioactive compounds that help in improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation.

Leaves: Leaves of Panax ginseng are utilized in decoctions for angina due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in protecting the heart from oxidative damage and reducing symptoms of angina.
remedy_recipe: Gather 8-10 grams of dried panax ginseng root and rinse it with clean water before use.
Cut the ginseng root into small pieces to increase its surface area for efficient extraction.
Steep 8-10 grams of ginseng pieces in 1 liter of boiling water for 1-2 hours.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Store the herbal decoction in the refrigerator and consume it within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with angina because it has been shown to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure, reducing the frequency and severity of anginal episodes. The diuretic properties of dandelion help to eliminate excess fluids from the body, further reducing fluid buildup that can contribute to angina. Additionally, the flavonoids present in dandelion may help to relax and dilate blood vessels, improving overall cardiovascular function and reducing the strain on the heart. This natural approach can provide relief for those suffering from angina without resorting to medication.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Dandelion contains cardiac glycosides, which can help to relax the heart muscle and reduce its workload, thereby alleviating symptoms of angina by reducing the frequency and severity of chest pain.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in dandelion decoctions have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect the cardiovascular system from damage and reduce inflammation that may exacerbate angina symptoms.

Inulin: Dandelion root contains a type of fructan called inulin, which has been shown to improve blood flow to the heart and reduce blood pressure, thereby helping to alleviate angina symptoms by increasing oxygen delivery to the heart muscle.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpene helps with angina by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which may reduce the severity of angina symptoms by minimizing inflammation in blood vessels.
Quercetin: This flavonoid phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, potentially alleviating angina symptoms.

Taraxasterol and other sesquiterpenes: These compounds have been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which can help widen blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the severity of angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate angina symptoms.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with angina.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried.
Clean the harvested taraxacum officinale roots and leaves thoroughly with lukewarm water.
Combine 1 part of taraxacum officinale roots and leaves with 5 parts of water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with angina because it has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and relax blood vessels. The decoction's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage cardiac tissue and contribute to angina symptoms. Additionally, the decoction's ability to dilate blood vessels can increase oxygen delivery to the heart, reducing chest pain and discomfort associated with angina. Regular consumption of elder decoctions may also help to strengthen the cardiovascular system, improving overall heart health.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Elderflower contains flavonoids, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing symptoms of angina by increasing oxygen delivery to the myocardium.
Saponins: Saponins in elderberry have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and prevent platelet aggregation, thereby alleviating angina attacks.

Volatile oils: Elderflower volatile oils contain sesquiterpenes, which have a vasodilatory effect, helping to widen blood vessels and improve perfusion of the heart muscle, reducing the frequency and severity of angina episodes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Sambucus nigra decoctions help with angina by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the heart muscle, thereby alleviating symptoms of angina.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid): Phenolic acids in Sambucus nigra decoctions help with angina by exerting antioxidant properties, which protect the heart from oxidative stress and inflammation, and may help improve cardiovascular function.

Triterpenoids (e.g., ursolic acid): Triterpenoids in Sambucus nigra decoctions help with angina by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress in the heart muscle, thereby alleviating symptoms of angina.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are commonly used to make decoctions for angina due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra are used for their vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to alleviate angina symptoms by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation.

Barks: The barks of Sambucus nigra are used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, thereby alleviating angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in late spring or early summer when they are in full bloom.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers and leaves by spreading them on a clean cloth for at least 24 hours.
Combine 20-30 grams of dried flowers and leaves in a clean glass container with 500 milliliters of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes in a covered container to allow the herbs to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle decoctions helps with angina because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds that have a profound impact on blood vessels. The decoction's ability to dilate coronary arteries and improve blood flow can significantly reduce the frequency and severity of anginal episodes. Additionally, stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory properties help to relax vascular smooth muscle, further enhancing blood vessel dilation and reducing overall symptoms of angina.

remedy_constituents: Nasunin: A polyphenolic flavonoid antioxidant found in Urtica dioica, nasunin helps with angina by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, thus improving blood flow and reducing cardiac strain.

Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound, quercetin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate angina by reducing inflammation and oxidative damage in blood vessels and the heart.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate angina symptoms.
Roots: The roots contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, which are believed to exert a protective effect on the heart.
Stems: The stems are a good source of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that may help soothe and calm the heart tissue, reducing angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh or dried leaves and stems of urtica dioica plants.
Clean the urtica dioica material by washing it with cold running water thoroughly.
Combine 30 grams of fresh or 15 grams of dried urtica dioica material with 1 liter of water.
Boil the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose decoctions helps with angina because of its unique composition, which includes flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These bioactive compounds have been shown to exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease and alleviate symptoms of angina. The decoction's vasodilatory effects can also increase blood flow to the heart, reducing the frequency and severity of anginal episodes. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Rosa damascena decoctions, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, thereby alleviating angina symptoms.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a polyphenolic compound found in Rosa damascena, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce vascular inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, thereby relieving angina symptoms.

Rosa damascena essential oil compounds (e.g., linalool and geraniol): These compounds have vasodilatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the severity and frequency of angina attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate angina symptoms.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that act as vasodilators, improving blood flow and reducing angina pain.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rosa damascena flowers at dawn when dew is still present on the petals about 1 cup dried flowers.
Steep the dried rosa damascena flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes with 1 cup flowers to 1 liter water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discard solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature and then refrigerate it for up to 2 days.
Use the cooled decoction as needed and discard any remaining liquid after 2 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry decoctions helps with angina because they are rich in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. The decoctions also contain flavonoids, which have been shown to relax blood vessels and improve the body's ability to regulate blood pressure. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, blueberry decoctions may help to alleviate angina symptoms by increasing oxygen delivery to the heart and reducing the frequency and severity of attacks. remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to angina by improving blood vessel function and reducing platelet aggregation.

Vitamin C: This essential vitamin helps maintain healthy blood vessels, improves circulation, and reduces the risk of atherosclerosis (the narrowing of arteries), all of which can contribute to angina relief.

Fiber: The soluble fiber content in blueberry decoctions helps bind to bile acids, reducing cholesterol levels and improving lipid profiles, which can help alleviate angina symptoms by reducing cardiac workload.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to angina symptoms by improving blood flow and reducing vascular constriction.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects, which can help improve blood flow to the heart muscle and reduce the severity of angina symptoms by lowering blood pressure and relaxing blood vessels.

Catechins: These phenolic compounds have vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the risk of angina by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation in the blood vessels, leading to improved heart function and reduced symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Vaccinium myrtillus are used to make decoctions for angina because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Buds: The buds of Vaccinium myrtillus are used to make decoctions for angina because they have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.

Leaves: The leaves of Vaccinium myrtillus are used to make decoctions for angina because they have been shown to have vasodilatory effects, helping to improve blood flow and reduce symptoms of angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh vaccinium myrtillus berries in late summer or early fall when they are ripe and dark purple.
Rinse the vaccinium myrtillus berries thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the vaccinium myrtillus berries with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly and cooled.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup to remove the solids.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Cornflower decoctions have been shown to exhibit antioxidant activity, which helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, a major contributor to angina symptoms.

Cardiac glycoside content: Cornflower contains cardiac glycosides, such as cymarin, which have been traditionally used to treat heart conditions, including angina, by regulating heart rhythm and improving blood flow.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoctions also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to angina symptoms by restricting blood flow to the heart.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Centaurea cyanus decoctions have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, alleviating angina symptoms.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Centaurea cyanus decoctions possess vasodilatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the severity of angina.

remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 20-30 fresh or dried flowers in the early morning or afternoon when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the flowers in a warm place for 1-2 weeks to prevent moisture from affecting their medicinal properties completely.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers with 1 cup of boiling water to make a strong decoction rapidly.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water thoroughly.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids before drinking the liquid immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort decoctions helps with angina because of its unique ability to improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the frequency and severity of anginal episodes. The herb's active compounds, hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to increase nitric oxide production in the body, which relaxes blood vessels and enhances blood flow. This natural vasodilation helps reduce the strain on the heart, allowing it to pump more efficiently and reducing symptoms of chest pain, shortness of breath, and fatigue associated with angina.
remedy_properties: Hypericin: This bioactive compound has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce platelet aggregation, which can help alleviate angina symptoms by increasing oxygen delivery to the heart.

Flavonoids: St. John's Wort contains various flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cardiac damage and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, further contributing to improved blood flow and reduced angina symptoms.

Hyperforin: This compound has been found to have a positive effect on heart rate and blood pressure regulation, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of anginal episodes by improving overall cardiovascular function.
remedy_constituents: Hyperoside: This flavonoid glycoside helps with angina by exerting a vasodilatory effect, which improves blood circulation and reduces the heart's workload.

Quercetin: As a polyphenolic compound, quercetin helps alleviate angina symptoms by scavenging free radicals, reducing inflammation, and preventing platelet aggregation, thus improving cardiovascular health.
Hypericin: This naphthodianthrone compound has antioxidant properties that help protect the heart from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are associated with angina development and progression.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in hyperoside and other flavonoids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular-protective properties.
Roots: Contain bioflavonoids and phenolic acids that help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system.
Flowers: Rich in nectar and flavonoids, including rutin, which may help improve blood flow and reduce symptoms of angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately one to two pounds of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the early morning.
Clean the hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove any debris or contaminants.
Combine one to two ounces of the cleaned hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves with four cups of filtered water in a pot.
Bring the water to a boil over high heat then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 to 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena decoctions helps with angina because its unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils possesses potent vasodilatory properties. These compounds relax blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart muscle and reducing symptoms of angina such as chest pain and shortness of breath. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning.
Combine 2 teaspoons of herb material with 2 cups of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep for 10-15 minutes before straining and discarding solids.
Consume 1 cup of the warm decoction three times daily as needed.
Store any remaining decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with angina because of its natural ability to dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow to the heart. The flavonoids present in fennel help to relax smooth muscle tissue, reducing spasm and constriction of blood vessels that can cause chest pain. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its benefits by reducing inflammation in the coronary arteries, further alleviating anginal symptoms. By promoting healthy cardiovascular function, fennel decoctions can provide effective relief from angina attacks.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Fennel decoction contains cardiac glycosides, such as anahydrine, which have a positive inotropic effect on the heart, increasing its contraction strength and improving blood circulation to the heart muscle, helping alleviate symptoms of angina.

Vasodilatory effects: Fennel's active compounds, including volatile oils like anethole and fenchol, can help dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving blood flow to the heart muscle, thereby alleviating anginal pain and reducing symptoms of coronary artery disease.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Fennel decoction's polyphenolic compounds and other bioactive molecules have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, both of which are believed to contribute to the development of angina.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are a popular choice for making decoctions due to their rich antioxidant and flavonoid content, which can help alleviate anginal symptoms.

Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a unique combination of essential oils and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, making them an effective ingredient in angina treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried fennel seeds from certified organic sources in amounts of one tablespoon per day.
Grind the dried fennel seeds into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for three minutes.
Combine the ground fennel powder with 250 milliliters of boiling water and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and discard.
Consume the resulting decoction immediately after preparation in divided doses three times daily for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with angina because of its ability to relax blood vessels, improve circulation, and reduce inflammation. The licorice root contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that have vasodilatory properties, which can help to open up constricted arteries and increase oxygen supply to the heart. Additionally, licorice's anti-inflammatory effects may also contribute to its ability to alleviate chest pain associated with angina. remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Licorice root contains cardiac glycosides, such as glycyrrhizin, which help to reduce the frequency and severity of anginal attacks by slowing down the heart rate and increasing blood flow to the heart.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root's anti-inflammatory compounds, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation in the coronary arteries, improving blood flow to the heart muscle and reducing the risk of angina symptoms.

Glycyrrhetic acid: Glycyrrhetic acid, a compound found in licorice root, has been shown to help relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which can reduce the frequency and severity of anginal episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in relieving chest pain associated with angina.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been used to treat cardiovascular diseases, including angina, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 30g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in cold water to remove impurities.
Boil the rinsed roots in 600ml of distilled water at 100°c for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for an additional 20 minutes to enhance extraction.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Allow the cooled decoction to stand at room temperature for 30 minutes before drinking.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silverleaf decoctions helps with angina because they contain flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to improve blood flow to the heart, reducing symptoms of angina. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties also help to relax blood vessels, allowing for better oxygenation of the heart muscle. Additionally, the antioxidants present in silverleaf decoctions may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, further contributing to a reduction in anginal symptoms.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic and vasodilatory effects: Silverleaf's cardiotonic and vasodilatory properties help to improve blood flow, reduce blood pressure, and increase oxygenation of the heart muscle, alleviating symptoms of angina.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions: Silverleaf's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to the development of angina.

Beta-adrenergic blocking activity: Silverleaf's beta-adrenergic blocking activity helps to slow down heart rate, lower blood pressure, and reduce cardiac workload, which can alleviate symptoms of angina.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, making them effective in alleviating angina symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, they are used to reduce oxidative stress and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried cistus x incanus leaves and flowers and transfer it into a clean glass jar.
Heat one cup of filtered water to a rolling boil in a stainless steel or enamel-coated pot.
Pour the boiling water over the measured cistus x incanus mixture in the jar, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Store the final decoction in an airtight glass bottle at room temperature for up to 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with angina because it possesses a unique combination of properties that can alleviate symptoms. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds present in chamomile help to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing chest pain associated with angina. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can also help to reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers for angina attacks, thereby providing a natural and soothing relief from this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and heart tissue, which may alleviate symptoms of angina.

Vasodilatory properties: The flavonoids present in chamomile decoctions, like quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the frequency and severity of anginal episodes.

Antioxidant activity: Chamomile's antioxidant compounds can help neutralize free radicals that can damage heart tissue and contribute to the development of cardiovascular disease, which may reduce the risk of angina attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation, making it effective in soothing anginal pain.
Flowers: Contain bisabolol, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce spasms in blood vessels and alleviate chest discomfort associated with angina.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers or three teaspoons of fresh flowers.
Combine the measured herbs with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the herbs to infuse and release their medicinal properties.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids.
Allow the cooled decoction to sit for at least one hour before consumption to allow the active compounds to settle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with angina because it has been traditionally used to soothe and relax the cardiovascular system. The mucilages present in marshmallows help to reduce inflammation and ease spasms in the blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing chest pain. Additionally, marshmallow's calming properties can also help to alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers of angina attacks. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, herbal marshmallow decoctions may provide relief from anginal episodes and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow decoctions contain mucilages, which form a protective film on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation in the chest and heart area, thereby alleviating anginal symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in marshmallow decoctions help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing the risk of cardiac events.

Cardiotonic and anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow decoctions have been shown to have cardiotonic effects, improving heart function and reducing inflammation in the coronary arteries, which can help alleviate anginal symptoms by improving blood flow to the heart.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with angina.
Buds: Marshmallow buds contain mucilages that can help to soothe and calm inflammation in the blood vessels, alleviating symptoms of angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis leaves and flowers, ensuring they are clean and free of debris.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried althaea officinalis per cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured herb with the boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Allow the decoction to cool before drinking, consuming up to three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with angina because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory properties. These compounds help to relax blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing symptoms of angina such as chest pain and shortness of breath. Additionally, the decoctions may also help to stabilize platelets and prevent blood clots from forming, further reducing the risk of cardiovascular events.
remedy_properties: Cardioprotective flavonoids: Flavonoids in Kansas Coneflower, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the cardiovascular system from damage and reduce the risk of heart disease.

Vasodilatory sesquiterpenes: Sesquiterpenes like α-humulene in Kansas Coneflower may help dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow to the heart muscle and reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks.

Anti-inflammatory phenolic acids: Phenolic acids present in Kansas Coneflower, such as caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, contributing to alleviation of angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, the roots of Kansas coneflower help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, making them effective in managing angina symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain antioxidant compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, which is beneficial for individuals with angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root and clean it thoroughly.
Crush the dried root into small pieces to increase its surface area for extraction.
Combine 5 grams of crushed echinacea root with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 20-25 minutes, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Store the decoction in an airtight container and take 1/2 cup three times a day before meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with angina because of its ability to relax the heart muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve blood circulation. The decoction's antispasmodic properties can help ease chest pain caused by angina, while its vasodilatory effects can increase blood flow to the heart muscle, reducing oxygen demand and alleviating symptoms. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce platelet aggregation and inflammation in the arteries, further contributing to improved cardiovascular function and reduced anginal episodes.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lavender's antispasmodic effects can help relax smooth muscle tissue in blood vessels, which may reduce spasms and improve blood flow to the heart, alleviating symptoms of angina.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to the development of angina.

Calcium channel blocker-like activity: Some studies suggest that lavender extracts may have calcium channel blocking effects, similar to certain medications used to treat angina, which can help improve blood flow and reduce cardiac workload.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, alleviating symptoms of angina.
Barks: The barks contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to relax blood vessels and reduce oxidative stress, making it an effective ingredient for treating angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia leaves or purchase dried material in appropriate quantities.
Rinse the harvested leaves with filtered water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Combine one tablespoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers or one ounce of fresh leaves with four cups of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Drink the decoction three times daily, two hours apart, after meals to alleviate angina symptoms effectively.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pink peony decoctions helps with angina because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, glycosides, and anthocyanins that work synergistically to improve cardiovascular health. The decoction's bioactive compounds help to relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving circulation to the heart muscle. This increased blood flow can alleviate anginal symptoms by reducing oxygen demand on the heart and improving overall cardiac function. Additionally, pink peony decoctions' antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, further contributing to its therapeutic benefits for angina sufferers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Pink peony decoctions have antioxidant compounds that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to the development of angina.

Cardiovascular protective property: The decoction's cardiovascular-protective effects are thought to be due to its ability to dilate blood vessels, improve blood flow, and lower blood pressure, all of which can help alleviate symptoms of angina.

Calcium-channel blocking property: Pink peony has been shown to possess calcium-channel blocking activity, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of anginal episodes by decreasing the workload on the heart.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peony roots have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat cardiovascular diseases, including angina, due to their vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried paeonia officinalis root bark in late summer or fall weighing about 20-30 grams.
Rinse the dried paeonia officinalis root bark thoroughly with pure cold water then chop it finely into small pieces.
Combine the chopped paeonia officinalis root bark with 4 cups of purified water in a large ceramic pot bringing to boil and then reduce heat.
Simmer the decoction for 30 minutes or longer under low heat allowing active compounds to infuse and diffuse slowly.
Strain the hot decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids and store in refrigerator.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to angina that are treated with herbal decoctions are coronary artery disease, hypertension, and heart failure. Herbal decoctions of ingredients such as hawthorn berries, turmeric, and ginger have been used traditionally to improve circulation, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms associated with these conditions. Decoctions may also be used to treat other cardiovascular issues like arrhythmias and chest pain.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with angina is a blend of Hawthorn (Crataegus oxycantha), Ginkgo biloba, and Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera). Hawthorn helps to improve blood flow to the heart, reducing symptoms of angina. Ginkgo biloba enhances circulation and reduces oxidative stress, while Ashwagandha calms the nervous system and reduces inflammation. This combination can help alleviate angina symptoms, improve cardiovascular health, and promote overall well-being. However, consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have a pre-existing medical condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba decoctions helps with angina because they improve blood flow to the heart by dilating blood vessels and reducing platelet aggregation. This increases oxygen delivery to the heart, reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks. The antioxidant properties of ginkgo biloba also help protect the heart against oxidative stress, which can contribute to angina. Additionally, ginkgo biloba's vasodilatory effects may help lower blood pressure and reduce the workload on the heart, further alleviating angina symptoms. Regular consumption of ginkgo biloba decoctions may help alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with angina by improving blood flow to the heart muscle, reducing oxidative stress, and inhibiting platelet aggregation.
Bilobalide: Help with angina by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart muscle, and relaxing blood vessel walls.
Ginkgolides: Help with angina by inhibiting platelet aggregation and improving blood flow to the heart muscle, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds contain ginkgolic acid, which may help reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Barks: Ginkgo bark contains flavonoids and terpenoids that may help improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 120-240 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in the fall.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks.
Grind 30-60 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powder in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain and discard the solids before serving the decoction warm or at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina decoctions helps with angina because they contain alkaloids that have a vasodilatory effect, which helps to relax and widen blood vessels. This increased blood flow reduces the workload on the heart, thereby alleviating the symptoms of angina. Additionally, the decoctions may help to lower blood pressure and reduce the frequency and severity of angina attacks. The antioxidant properties of Rauvolfia serpentina may also contribute to its cardiotonic and vasodilatory effects, making it a potentially beneficial herbal remedy for managing angina.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids help with angina by reducing blood pressure and heart rate, thus easing the workload on the heart and improving blood flow to the coronary arteries.
Reserpine: Reserpine helps with angina by preventing the release of norepinephrine, a neurotransmitter that can increase heart rate and blood pressure, thereby reducing the risk of angina attacks.

Serotonin: Serotonin helps with angina by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety and stress, and improving overall cardiovascular health, all of which can contribute to a reduced risk of angina attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Alkaloids found in the roots are primarily used to make decoctions for angina, providing relief from symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves contain reserpine, an alkaloid used in decoctions to treat angina by dilating blood vessels.
Barks: The barks of Rauvolfia serpentina contain various alkaloids, including yohimbine, which is used in decoctions to treat angina.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of dried root of rauvolfia serpentina and clean it thoroughly with lukewarm water for 10 minutes.
Crush the cleaned rauvolfia root into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 20 minutes.
Mix 3 grams of the rauvolfia powder with 500 milliliters of water in a saucepan and boil it gently for 10 minutes.
Reduce the heat to simmer and let the decoction cook for an additional 20 minutes to enhance the bioavailability.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng decoctions helps with angina because they improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and promoting vasodilation. The active compounds in ginseng, such as ginsenosides and panaxans, have been shown to increase blood flow and reduce blood pressure. This leads to a decrease in the frequency and severity of angina attacks. Additionally, ginseng decoctions may also improve oxygenation of the heart muscle, allowing it to function more efficiently and reducing the strain on the heart. remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These ginsenosides help with angina by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation, which in turn reduces the frequency and severity of angina attacks.
Glycosides: Glycosides in Panax ginseng decoctions help with angina by improving myocardial function, reducing oxidative stress, and enhancing the heart's ability to pump blood efficiently.

Saponins: Saponins in Panax ginseng decoctions help with angina by reducing blood pressure, improving lipid profiles, and inhibiting platelet aggregation, all of which contribute to reduced angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: As the primary medicinal part of Panax ginseng, roots contain the highest concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to help alleviate angina symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Panax ginseng are also used to make decoctions for angina, as they contain a lower concentration of ginsenosides compared to the roots, but still offer potential benefits.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, also known as underground stem parts, of Panax ginseng are sometimes used in decoctions for angina, as they may contain a mix of ginsenosides and other compounds that could help alleviate symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 6-9 grams of dried panax ginseng root and rinse it under cold running water to remove impurities.
Boil 2 cups of water in a saucepan and add the rinsed panax ginseng root for 10-15 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 30-40 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a separate container to remove the root fragments.
Store the herbal panax ginseng decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna decoctions helps with angina because they contain flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which have been shown to improve cardiovascular function and reduce inflammation. These compounds help to improve blood flow to the heart, reduce blood pressure, and prevent platelet aggregation. By doing so, Crataegus monogyna decoctions may help alleviate the symptoms of angina, such as chest pain and shortness of breath, and improve overall cardiovascular health. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with angina by improving heart function, reducing inflammation, and preventing platelet aggregation, which in turn reduces the risk of blood clots and cardiac arrhythmias.

Phenolic acids: Contribute to angina relief by inhibiting the production of nitric oxide synthase, an enzyme involved in the formation of nitric oxide, a potent vasodilator that helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart.

Glycosides: Help with angina by exerting a direct effect on the heart, increasing its contractility and reducing its workload, while also improving blood flow and oxygen delivery to the cardiac muscle.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids, which help to improve blood circulation and reduce symptoms of angina.
Leaves: They are used as they contain various compounds that help to dilate blood vessels, reducing the risk of angina attacks.
Buds: They are used due to their ability to improve blood circulation and lower blood pressure, both of which can help to alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 4-6 parts of crataegus monogyna leaves and 2-3 parts of its flowers in a clean container.
Combine the collected herbs with 1 liter of cold water in a large saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce heat to low.
Simmer the decoction for 10-15 minutes then strain it using a cheesecloth or a fine mesh.
Store the prepared decoction in a clean glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale decoctions helps with angina because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. Shogaols: Shogaols have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, ultimately contributing to improved blood flow and reduced angina symptoms.

Zingiberene: Zingiberene, a sesquiterpene, has been shown to have a vasodilatory effect, which can help widen blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart muscle, thereby reducing angina symptoms and improving overall cardiovascular function.

Stems: The stems are used for their ability to improve blood circulation and reduce blood pressure, which helps to alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-25 grams of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes for a decoction.
Boil 1 liter of water in a saucepan over high heat for 5 minutes.
Add the dried zingiber officinale rhizomes to the boiling water and steep for 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve to remove the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica decoctions helps with angina because it improves circulation and reduces inflammation in the blood vessels. remedy_recipe: Rinse 30-40g of dried centella asiatica leaves and stems with cold water to remove impurities.
Boil 1 liter of water in a saucepan over high heat for 5-7 minutes.
Add the rinsed centella asiatica to the boiling water and stir gently for 3 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid reduces slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis decoctions helps with angina because they contain compounds that dilate blood vessels, improve blood flow, and reduce inflammation. remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of antioxidants and volatile oils, which help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow.

Stems: They are used due to their ability to provide a sustained release of antioxidants and volatile oils, helping to reduce oxidative stress and improve heart health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves or 10-15 grams of dried leaves at peak potency.
Chop the rosmarinus officinalis leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped rosmarinus officinalis with 1 liter of boiling water in a large heat-resistant container.
Steep the rosmarinus officinalis mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow flavors and compounds to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container for consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Crataegus oxyacantha decoctions helps with angina because they contain flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce the workload on the heart. These compounds help to relax blood vessels and improve coronary circulation, allowing more oxygen to reach the heart muscle. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Crataegus oxyacantha decoctions help to protect the heart from oxidative stress and damage, contributing to improved cardiovascular function and reduced symptoms of angina. Quercetin: Quercetin contributes to angina relief by inhibiting platelet aggregation and reducing inflammation in blood vessels, which in turn improves blood circulation and lowers blood pressure.

remedy_recipe: Gather 30-60 grams of dried c oxyacantha flowers and leaves for the decoction.
Combine the herb with 500 ml of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.

Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis decoctions helps with angina because of its vasodilatory properties, which aid in relaxing the blood vessels and improving blood flow to the heart. This increased blood flow reduces the workload on the heart, allowing it to function more efficiently and decrease the frequency of angina attacks. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of valerian root help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, further contributing to its potential benefits in managing angina symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It helps alleviate angina symptoms by reducing anxiety and stress, which contribute to heart rate and blood pressure increases.
Valeranone: It has a sedative effect, which helps to lower the heart rate and reduce the frequency of angina attacks.
Acidic isovaleramide: It has vasodilatory properties, which help to improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of valerenic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties that help alleviate angina symptoms.
Roots: They contain valerianic acid, which helps in relaxing the muscles and improving blood flow to the heart, thus reducing angina pain.
Roots: They have been traditionally used to treat heart-related issues, including angina, due to their ability to dilate blood vessels and improve heart function.
remedy_recipe: Dry the valeriana officinalis roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 gram of the ground valeriana officinalis powder with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Coleus forskohlii decoctions helps with angina because they increase blood flow and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. The active compound, forskolin, relaxes the smooth muscle in the blood vessels, allowing more oxygen and nutrients to reach the heart. This leads to improved heart function and reduced symptoms of angina, such as chest pain and shortness of breath. Regular consumption of Coleus forskohlii decoctions may also lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health. As a result, it can help alleviate angina symptoms and promote a healthier heart.
remedy_constituents: Forskolin: Helps with angina by increasing the production of cyclic adenosine monophosphate (cAMP), a molecule that relaxes the muscles in the heart and improves blood flow, thereby reducing chest pain and pressure.
Dihydrotanshinone: Helps with angina by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels, which can contribute to angina symptoms, and improving cardiovascular health.

Tanshinone: Helps with angina by acting as a vasodilator, which means it helps to widen the blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the workload on the heart and alleviating symptoms of angina.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of forskolin, a compound that helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to stimulate the heart and improve blood circulation, which helps to alleviate symptoms of angina.
Rhyzomes: They are used because they contain a high amount of forskolin, which has vasodilatory properties that help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh coleus forskohlii leaves in the morning when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Dry the harvested coleus forskohlii leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Crush 4-6 grams of dried coleus forskohlii leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction after 5-7 minutes and discard the solids before drinking the liquid.
